notes.title: Machine Learning — Core Concepts
notes.summary: Supervised vs unsupervised vs reinforcement learning, model evaluation, and overfitting.
points: Supervised learning: learn a mapping from inputs to labelled outputs.
Unsupervised: find structure in unlabelled data (clustering, dimensionality reduction).
Overfitting: model memorises training data; fix with regularisation, dropout, or more data.
Bias-variance trade-off: high bias = underfitting; high variance = overfitting.
Evaluation: accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC — choose based on the problem.

notes.title: On-Device ML for Android
notes.summary: Running inference on-device using TFLite, ONNX, and feature extraction with TarsosDSP.
points: TensorFlow Lite runs quantised models efficiently on Android CPU/GPU/NPU.
ONNX Runtime for Android is an alternative for models trained in PyTorch.
TarsosDSP is a Java/Kotlin audio analysis library — extracts BPM, pitch, energy.
Keep inference at index time (not real-time) to avoid battery drain.
k-NN similarity search on feature vectors is simple, fast, and explainable.

notes.title: Large Language Models — Key Ideas
notes.summary: How transformers work, attention mechanisms, and practical usage via APIs.
points: Transformer architecture: encoder-decoder or decoder-only (GPT-style).
Attention = weighted sum of values, where weights come from query-key similarity.
Prompt engineering: be specific, give examples, break tasks into steps.
Temperature controls randomness; top-p controls diversity of sampling.
Fine-tuning vs RAG: fine-tuning bakes knowledge in; RAG retrieves it at runtime.

notes.title: Python — Things I Always Forget
notes.summary: Walrus operator, dataclasses, context managers, and other useful patterns.
points: Walrus operator := assigns and returns: `if (n := len(a)) > 10:`.
dataclasses: `@dataclass` auto-generates __init__, __repr__, __eq__.
Context managers: implement __enter__ and __exit__, or use @contextmanager.
functools.lru_cache: memoises function results. Use maxsize=None for unlimited.
pathlib.Path is almost always better than os.path for file operations.

notes.title: Threading vs Multiprocessing in Python
notes.summary: When to use threads, when to use processes, and the GIL explained simply.
points: GIL (Global Interpreter Lock) prevents true parallel CPU execution in threads.
Use threading for I/O-bound tasks: network requests, file reads, UI updates.
Use multiprocessing for CPU-bound tasks: number crunching, image processing.
asyncio is best for many concurrent I/O-bound tasks with low overhead.
In Kivy, always run blocking work in a thread; use Clock.schedule_once to update UI.

notes.title: KivyMD Patterns That Work
notes.summary: Practical patterns for building real Android apps with KivyMD 1.2.x.
points: MDBottomSheet: set bg_color or it renders transparent on some devices.
Thread to fetch data, then use Clock.schedule_once(lambda dt: update_ui(), 0).
MDRecycleView with RecycleBoxLayout for long lists — avoids rebuilding widgets.
app.theme_cls.theme_style = 'Dark' must be set before any screen is built.
StringProperty and ListProperty trigger UI updates automatically — use them.

notes.title: Indus Valley Civilisation
notes.summary: Chalcolithic farming communities and the Harappan urban phase — key facts.
points: IVC flourished approx. 2600–1900 BCE in present-day Pakistan and northwest India.
Major sites: Mohenjo-daro, Harappa, Dholavira, Lothal, Rakhigarhi.
Features: planned cities, advanced drainage, standardised weights and measures.
Script remains undeciphered — a major unsolved problem in archaeology.
Decline likely due to combination of climate change, river shifts, and migration.

notes.title: Kohinoor Diamond — History
notes.summary: Origin, trail of ownership, and the ongoing repatriation debate.
points: Originated in the Golconda mines of the Deccan, possibly 13th century.
Passed through Kakatiya, Delhi Sultanate, Mughals, Nader Shah, Sikh Empire.
Came to the British Crown after the 1849 annexation of Punjab.
Currently part of the Crown Jewels at the Tower of London.
India, Pakistan, Afghanistan, and Iran have all claimed repatriation rights.

notes.title: Indian Political Geography — Delimitation
notes.summary: Delimitation process, the 2026 deadline, and political implications for South India.
points: Delimitation: redrawing of Lok Sabha and state assembly constituency boundaries.
Based on population — last delimitation was 1971 census data (frozen in 2002).
2026 deadline: freeze on seat allocation ends; new delimitation can begin post-2026 census.
South Indian states fear losing seats to high-population north because they controlled population growth better.
Article 82 and 170 govern Lok Sabha and state assembly delimitation respectively.

notes.title: Physical Geography — Basics
notes.summary: Plate tectonics, river systems, and climate classification.
points: Plate tectonics: Earth's lithosphere is divided into plates that move on the asthenosphere.
Convergent plates → mountains/trenches; divergent → ridges/rifts; transform → earthquakes.
Himalayas formed by collision of Indian and Eurasian plates — still rising ~5mm/year.
Köppen climate classification: A (tropical), B (arid), C (temperate), D (continental), E (polar).
India's rivers: Himalayan (perennial, snow-fed) vs Peninsular (seasonal, rain-fed).

notes.title: Systems Over Goals
notes.summary: Why building repeatable systems beats setting ambitious targets.
points: Goals are destinations; systems are the vehicle. You need both, but systems win long-term.
Reduce friction for good habits: if starting is hard, you won't start.
Time-blocking > to-do lists: assign tasks to specific time slots.
Weekly review: what worked, what didn't, what's next week's priority?
Shipping > perfecting: a good-enough thing that exists beats a perfect thing that doesn't.

notes.title: Learning Efficiently
notes.summary: Active recall, spaced repetition, and the Feynman technique.
points: Active recall: test yourself instead of re-reading. Retrieval strengthens memory.
Spaced repetition: review material at increasing intervals (Anki implements this).
Feynman technique: explain a concept simply. Where you stumble = gaps to fill.
Interleaving: mix topics during study. Harder short-term, better long-term retention.
Output > input: writing, building, and teaching beat consuming alone.
